question: Molecular assays for the diagnosis of sepsis in neonates
source: CLEF TAR 2018 Task 2 (Testing), content-level qrels — finally-included studies of Cochrane review CD011926.

protocol.seeds: none — proceed under the no-seed workflow
protocol.framework: diagnostic (PIRD / diagnostic PICO). Index test = molecular / nucleic-acid amplification assays; target condition = neonatal sepsis / bloodstream infection.
protocol.essential_concepts: neonatal sepsis / bloodstream infection; molecular / nucleic-acid amplification assay. Decide essential block structure yourself per the skill's anti-pattern guidance (prefer fewer AND blocks).
protocol.optional_blocks: DECLINE all optional secondary AND blocks — no diagnostic-accuracy/outcome block, no separate reference-standard block.
protocol.limits: none (no date, language, species, or age limits)
protocol.final_cleanup: remove exact duplicate terms and genuinely zero-hit phrases (after ruling out typos); keep all recall-bearing terms; apply automatically without asking.

strategy_provenance: Hand-built 3-block high-sensitivity PubMed strategy authored for this fixture as a scoring baseline. NOT the published Cochrane/Ovid search (that is in Ovid syntax and would need translation). Replace with skill-generated output in Phase 2.
notes: Diagnostic-test-accuracy review. Gold set = 29 included studies. Some may predate or fall outside PubMed; the harness reports reachable vs. unreachable separately.
